title: Add arrow-drawing animation to OAuth sequence diagram (currently instant)
description: OAuthSequenceDiagram (SecurityModule.tsx:198-351) renders arrows instantly via SVG <line> and <polygon>. No animation at all (VA2=0). Add: when stepping to a new message, animate the arrow drawing from sender to receiver over 300ms using SVG stroke-dasharray/stroke-dashoffset animation. This makes protocol flows feel ALIVE — messages visibly travel between actors. Reference: Mermaid.js sequence diagram animations.
acceptanceCriteria: New arrows animate drawing from left-to-right (or right-to-left) over 300ms
Self-referencing arrows animate the curved path
Previous arrows remain static (only new arrow animates)
Animation uses ease-out easing for natural deceleration

title: Add actor icons to OAuth and Web Attacks sequence diagrams
description: Both sequence diagrams use plain text rectangles for column headers ('User Agent', 'Client', 'Auth Server'). Replace with: User Agent → person icon + label, Client → app/laptop icon + label, Auth Server → shield/lock icon + label, Resource Server → database/server icon + label, Attacker → skull/warning icon + label. Use lucide-react icons (already imported). This adds visual identity and makes the diagrams instantly recognizable.
acceptanceCriteria: Each column header has an appropriate lucide-react icon above the label
Icons are consistent between OAuth and Web Attacks diagrams
Icons visible at all viewport sizes (scale with SVG viewBox)
Visual identity matches other Architex modules

title: Add DH paint-mixing animation (smooth color blend transition between swatches)
description: DH paint swatches (SecurityModule.tsx:673-707) appear instantly via `transition-opacity`. The paint MIXING analogy deserves a MIXING animation. When the shared secret is computed (steps 5-6), add a smooth CSS/SVG animation showing two color swatches merging into the combined color over 500ms. Use CSS mix-blend-mode or SVG gradient animation. Reference: 3Blue1Brown color mixing animations. This would make the best visualization in the module even more memorable.
acceptanceCriteria: Paint swatches smoothly blend colors when mixing occurs (steps 5-6)
Animation takes 400-600ms with ease-in-out easing
Blend animation respects prefers-reduced-motion
Final shared secret color (green) appears as the result of mixing

title: Add ShiftRows sliding animation to AES visualization
description: AES ShiftRows step (SecurityModule.tsx:988-1010) shows the matrix AFTER shifting — rows appear in new positions instantly. Add sliding animation: row 1 slides left 1 position over 200ms, row 2 slides left 2, row 3 slides left 3. Row 0 stays put. Use CSS transform: translateX() with transition. This makes the 'shift' operation VISIBLE rather than just described in text. Reference: AESvisual Demo mode animations.
acceptanceCriteria: When ShiftRows step is active, row cells animate sliding left
Each row slides by its row-index positions (row 1 = 1 cell, row 2 = 2, row 3 = 3)
Animation takes 200-400ms with ease-out
Cells that wrap around appear from the right side

title: Replace all hardcoded SVG hex colors with CSS custom properties
description: 40+ hardcoded hex values across OAuth, Web Attacks, Rate Limiting, and Encryption SVGs (see mega audit D2 table). These bypass the Tailwind/CSS variable system and prevent dark/light theme switching. Replace with CSS custom properties applied via SVG `style` attribute: `fill: var(--sec-svg-header-bg)`, `stroke: var(--sec-svg-border)`, etc. Define variables in the module's root or in Tailwind config.
acceptanceCriteria: Zero hardcoded hex colors in SVG elements (rect, line, text, polygon)
All SVG colors reference CSS custom properties
Visual appearance in dark theme matches current design exactly
Colors automatically adapt if light theme is added

title: Add pulse/glow effect to current step elements across all visualizers
description: 8 of 11 visualizations have VA6=0 (no state transitions). Active elements just change opacity or color instantly. Add a subtle pulse or glow animation to the CURRENT step element across all visualizers: (1) OAuth: current arrow gets a brief glow, (2) DH: current panel gets a border pulse, (3) AES: current cells get a brief scale pulse, (4) HTTPS: current phase block gets a left-border animation. Use CSS @keyframes or Tailwind animate-pulse/animate-ping. Keep subtle — 1-2 pulses, not continuous.
acceptanceCriteria: Current step element has a subtle visual distinction beyond just color change
Pulse animation plays once on step change (not continuous loop)
Animation is <500ms and doesn't interfere with readability
Respects prefers-reduced-motion
At least 6 of 11 visualizations have this treatment

title: Convert CORS visualization from card list to flowchart with decision diamonds
description: CORS currently renders as a vertical list of cards. The actual CORS algorithm is a DECISION TREE — each step is a yes/no branch. Convert to a proper flowchart: diamond shapes for decisions ('Same origin?', 'Simple request?', 'Preflight needed?'), rectangular shapes for actions ('Send OPTIONS', 'Send actual request'), with directed edges showing the flow. Pass=green path, Fail=red path terminating in a red X. Reference: MDN CORS flowchart diagram, AWS CORS explanation diagram.
acceptanceCriteria: CORS visualization renders as a proper flowchart (diamonds, rectangles, directed edges)
Decision nodes clearly show the condition being checked
Yes/No paths are visually distinct (green/red or solid/dashed)
Failed flow terminates at a red 'CORS Blocked' node
Current flow state highlighted during step-through

title: Add animated bucket fill/drain visualization for Rate Limiting
description: Rate limiting uses 'bucket' metaphors but shows no bucket. Add an SVG bucket visual for each algorithm: Token Bucket shows a bucket with tokens (circles) being consumed and refilling. Leaky Bucket shows water level rising (requests) and draining through a hole at the bottom. Sliding Window shows a window frame sliding across a timeline. These make the NAMES of the algorithms into VISUAL explanations. Reference: Stripe rate limiter blog diagrams.
acceptanceCriteria: Token Bucket shows visual bucket with token circles being consumed/refilled
Leaky Bucket shows water level animation with drain at bottom
Sliding Window shows a moving window frame on the timeline
Animations update in real-time as requests are processed
Visual bucket state matches the numeric values shown

title: Make DDoS simulation parameters user-configurable with sliders
description: DDoS simulation has hardcoded serverCapacity=200 and mitigationThreshold=250 (DDoSSimulationVisualizer.tsx:142-143). SR2=0/3. Add slider controls: server capacity (100-1000), mitigation threshold (150-500), attack intensity multiplier (0.5x-3x). Add presets: 'Small server' (capacity=100, threshold=150), 'Enterprise' (capacity=500, threshold=300), 'CDN-protected' (capacity=2000, threshold=500). This transforms a fixed slideshow into an interactive simulation.
acceptanceCriteria: 3 slider controls for server capacity, mitigation threshold, and attack intensity
Simulation updates in real-time when sliders change
At least 3 presets with descriptive names
Traffic bars and server load gauge reflect new parameters immediately

title: Add entry animation for all visualizations (fade-in + slide-up on topic switch)
description: When switching topics, the new canvas appears instantly. Add a subtle entry animation: canvas content fades in from 0 to 1 opacity over 200ms with a 12px slide-up (transform: translateY(12px) → translateY(0)). This adds polish and makes topic switching feel smooth. Use CSS transition on a wrapper div. Reference: Brilliant.org lesson transitions.
acceptanceCriteria: Switching topics triggers a fade-in + slide-up animation on the canvas
Animation is 150-250ms with ease-out easing
Previous canvas disappears instantly (no overlap)
Respects prefers-reduced-motion

title: Add Encryption data packet travel animation (SVG path animation)
description: Encryption canvas has a static data packet between Alice and Bob (SecurityModule.tsx:2527-2545) with only an opacity pulse `<animate>`. Replace with: when data travels over the network, animate the packet moving along the line from Alice to Bob (or vice versa) using SVG `<animateMotion>` along a path. When encrypted, the packet shows a lock icon and red color. When plaintext, it shows an open icon and green color. Reference: network packet animation visualizations.
acceptanceCriteria: Data packet visually travels from sender to receiver along the network line
Encrypted packets show lock icon and distinct color
Plaintext packets show different visual treatment
Animation takes 400-600ms per direction
Travel animation triggers on step advance, not continuously

title: Add Certificate Chain visual 'lock' animation on successful verification
description: When the full certificate chain verifies successfully (all steps valid), add a satisfying 'lock closed' animation: a padlock icon at the top of the chain animates from open to closed with a spring bounce. This provides visual closure — 'the chain is verified, the connection is secure.' On failure scenarios (expired/revoked), show the padlock staying open with a red X. Reference: Apple WWDC security presentation animations.
acceptanceCriteria: Padlock icon appears at the top of the cert chain visualization
On full validation success, padlock animates from open → closed with spring bounce
On failure, padlock stays open with red X overlay
Animation is 300-500ms with spring easing
